---
title: "modelling trade-off"
output: html_document
---

```{r}
library(lme4)
library(purrr)
library(broom)
library(MASS)
library(here)
library(dplyr)
library(car)
```

LABORATORY-RAISED MOTHS
```{r}
results <- read.csv(here("data", "clean data", "results.csv"), colClasses = c(sex = "character"))

#make maternal ID a factor
results$maternalID <- as.factor(results$maternalID)

#make a treatment combination column, filter to only include ambient, warm and hot larval temp
results <- results %>%
  mutate(treatment_combination = paste(larval_treatment, pupal_treatment, sep = "_"))  %>%
  filter(larval_treatment %in% c("ambient", "warm", "hot")) %>%
  mutate(
    larval_treatment = factor(larval_treatment, levels = c("ambient", "warm","hot")),
    pupal_treatment = factor(pupal_treatment, levels = c("ambient", "cool","mean","warm","hot"))) %>%
  mutate(weight_mg = weight * 1000)  %>%
  mutate(weight_mg_centered = weight_mg - mean(weight_mg, na.rm = TRUE))

 
```

```{r}
#tests whether the relationship between egg size and clutch size depends on larval treatment
#tests whether the relationshp between egg size and clutch size is dependent on pupal treatment
#the interactions allows for different slopes for each larval treatment, and for different slopes depending on pupal treatment
#so i have both random intercepts and random slope for each treatment
lm2 <- lmer(egg_mean_1A ~ clutch_size * larval_treatment + clutch_size * pupal_treatment + weight_mg + (1|maternalID), data = results)
summary(lm2)
#all interaction terms non-significant
AIC(lm2)
#AIC = -1465

confint(lm2)


#likelihood ratio for both interaction terms
lm2_no_clutch_size <- update(lm2, . ~ . - clutch_size:larval_treatment - clutch_size:pupal_treatment)
anova(lm2, lm2_no_clutch_size)

#likelihood ratio test for female mass
lm2_no_weight <- update(lm2, . ~ . - larval_treatment - weight_mg)
anova(lm2, lm2_no_weight)
#female mass not a signficant predictor of egg size

#maternal ID explains how much of the variance
4.77e-05/(4.77e-05 + 1.15e-04)
#maternal ID explains 29% of the variance in egg size

#checks for multicollinearity
vif(lm2)

```


WILD MOTHS

```{r}
results <- read.csv(here("data", "clean data", "results.csv"), colClasses = c(sex = "character"))

results_wild <- results %>%
  filter(pupal_treatment == "wild")

#cant include pupal treament, larval treatment, weight (i didn't weigh them, oops), or maternalID - I don't have any of this information
lm1 <- lm(egg_mean_1A ~ clutch_size, data = results_wild)
summary(lm1)
#non significant effect of clutch size on egg size

confint(lm1)
```
